stage1.label: Stage 1
stage1.description: A few quick questions to get started – these should feel familiar!

questions.text: Which of the following is a key benefit of MBV's van range over a standard passenger vehicle conversion?
options: Lower fuel consumption in all conditions
Purpose-built load floor and increased payload capacity
Smaller turning circle for urban delivery
Lower list price across all variants

questions.text: Which MBV van model sits in the large van segment?
Which feature is standard across the MBV Sprinter range and is a key differentiator vs competitors?
What does the 'e' prefix signify in models such as the eSprinter and eVito?
Which competitor is most frequently identified as MBV's primary rival in the large van segment?
When speaking to a customer about total cost of ownership, which of the following should you highlight as an MBV advantage?
options: Lower upfront list price vs all competitors
Residual values and long-term reliability reducing overall fleet costs
Free servicing for the first 3 years
No road tax on any MBV van

questions.text: Which body style is available on the Vito that is NOT typically offered by all direct competitors?

stage2.label: Stage 2
stage2.description: These questions are a little more in-depth. We're looking for your honest thoughts – there are no wrong answers here.

questions.text: In your own words, how would you explain the key advantages of the MBV eSprinter to a customer who is considering switching from a diesel large van?
Describe a situation (real or hypothetical) where you would use a competitor comparison to support a customer's buying decision. Which competitor would you focus on and why?
What aspect of the Product & Competition training did you find most useful in your day-to-day role, and how have you applied it?
Is there any area of MBV's product range or a specific competitor that you feel you need more knowledge or support on? Please describe.
A fleet customer asks you to compare the Sprinter against the Ford Transit on payload. Which of the following is the most accurate and useful response?
options: The Sprinter always has a higher payload than the Transit
Payload varies by body style and configuration – I'd recommend we look at the specific variants that match your use case
They are identical in payload capacity
The Transit has a higher payload on all variants

questions.text: What do you think could be improved about the Product & Competition training programme itself? Your feedback will directly shape future sessions.
